2603.16551.title: CompDiff: Hierarchical Compositional Diffusion for Fair and Zero-Shot Intersectional Medical Image Generation
2603.16551.paper_summary: Generative models are increasingly used to augment medical imaging datasets for fairer AI. Yet a key assumption often goes unexamined: that generators themselves produce equally high-quality images across demographic groups. Models trained on imbalanced data can inherit these imbalances, yielding degraded synthesis quality for rare subgroups and struggling with demographic intersections absent from training. We refer to this as the imbalanced generator problem. Existing remedies such as loss reweighting operate at the optimization level and provide limited benefit when training signal is scarce or absent for certain combinations. We propose CompDiff, a hierarchical compositional diffusion framework that addresses this problem at the representation level. A dedicated Hierarchical Conditioner Network (HCN) decomposes demographic conditioning, producing a demographic token concatenated with CLIP embeddings as cross-attention context. This structured factorization encourages parameter sharing across subgroups and supports compositional generalization to rare or unseen demographic intersections. Experiments on chest X-rays (MIMIC-CXR) and fundus images (FairGenMed) show that CompDiff compares favorably against both standard fine-tuning and FairDiffusion across image quality (FID: 64.3 vs. 75.1), subgroup equity (ES-FID), and zero-shot intersectional generalization (up to 21% FID improvement on held-out intersections). Downstream classifiers trained on CompDiff-generated data also show improved AUROC and reduced demographic bias, suggesting that architectural design of demographic conditioning is an important and underexplored factor in fair medical image generation. Code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CompDiff-6FE6.
2603.16551.paper_summary_zh: 生成模型越來越多地用於增強醫學影像數據集，以實現更公平的人工智慧。<lb/>然而，一個關鍵的假設往往未被檢視：生成器本身在不同人口群體中產生的圖像質量是否相同。<lb/>在不平衡數據上訓練的模型可能會繼承這些不平衡，導致對於稀有子群體的合成質量下降，並在訓練中缺失的人口交集上表現不佳。<lb/>我們將此稱為不平衡生成器問題。<lb/>現有的補救措施如損失重加權在優化層面運作，當某些組合的訓練信號稀缺或缺失時，提供的好處有限。<lb/>我們提出了CompDiff，一個層次化的組合擴散框架，旨在在表示層面解決此問題。<lb/>專門的層次化條件網絡（HCN）對人口條件進行分解，生成與CLIP嵌入串聯的人口標記作為交叉注意力上下文。<lb/>這種結構化的因式分解鼓勵跨子群體的參數共享，並支持對稀有或未見人口交集的組合泛化。<lb/>在胸部X光（MIMIC-CXR）和眼底圖像（FairGenMed）上的實驗顯示，CompDiff在圖像質量（FID：64.3對75.1）、子群體公平性（ES-FID）和零樣本交叉泛化（對保留交集的FID改善高達21%）方面，與標準微調和FairDiff相比表現良好。<lb/>在CompDiff生成的數據上訓練的下游分類器也顯示出改進的AUROC和降低的人口偏見，這表明人口條件的架構設計是公平醫學影像生成中一個重要且未被充分探討的因素。<lb/>代碼可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/CompDiff-6FE6 獲得。
2603.16551.author: Mahmoud Ibrahim et.al.
2603.16551.authors: Mahmoud Ibrahim, Bart Elen, Chang Sun, Gokhan Ertaylan, Michel Dumontier
